folio.label: 167v
folio.l:
Lo quod Dame Prudence , how ligútly is euäy man enclyned , to his
owene desirö , & to his owene plesance

¢ Certes q¶ she , æe wordes
of the phisiciens , ne shulden nat han ben vnderstonden in that
wise

¢ For certes wykkednesse is nat contrie to wykkednesse ,
ne vengeance to vengeance , ne wrongö to wrongö , but æei ben
semblable

¢ And æäfore a vengeance is nat warisshed , by a noæer
vengeance , ne a wrongö by a nother wrongö ,

but euäycú of hem ,
encrescetú & aggreggetú other

¢ But certes the wordes of the phi-
siciens , shulden ben vnderstonde in this wyse ,

For good & wyk-
kednesse , ben two contries , And pees & werre , vengeance & suffrau~-
ce , discord & acord , & manye othere thynges

But certes wykked-
nesse , shal be warisshed , by goodnesse , discord by a cord , werre by
pees , & so fortú of othere thynges

¢ And here to acordetú Sey~t
Poule the Apostle in many places ,

he seitú , ne ©eldetú nat harm
for harm , ne wykked speche , for wykked speche ,

but do wel to hi~ ,
æ dotú to the harm , & blisse hi~ , æ seitú to the harm

¢ And in
manye othere places , he amonestetú pees & acord

¢ But now
wil I speke to ©ow , of æe cou~seil , whicú æ was ©euen to ©ow ,
by the men of lawe , & the wise folk ,

æ seiden alle by oon acord
as ©e han herd bifore

That ouä alle thynges , ©e shuln do ©ourö
diligence , to kepe ©oure ¹sone , & to warnestore ©oure hous ,

And
seiden also , æ in this cas , ©ow ougúte for to werke ful auysely ,
& witú gret deliberacou~

¢ And sire as to the first poynt , æat
touchetú to the kepyngö , of ©oure ¹sone ,

©e shuln vnderstonde , æ
he æ hatú werre , shal euäe more deuoutly & mekely preyen bi-
forn~ alle thynges ,

æ Iú~us cést of his mäcy , wil han hi~ , in his
¸teccion , & ben his souereyn helpyngö at his nede ,

for certes in
this world , æäe is no wigút , æ may be cou~seilled &ne&ne kept suffice-
antly , w oute the kepyng , of oure lord iúu cést

¢ To this sen-
tence , acordetú , the ¸phete Dauid , æ seitú ,

If god ne kepe æe Citee ,
in Idel waketú he , æ kepetú it

¢ Now sire , than shuln ©e co~-
mitte the kepyngö of ©oure ¹sone , to ©oure trewe frendes , æ ben
appreued and I knowe ,

& of he~ shuln ©e axen helpe , ©oure ¹sone
for to kepe , For Catou~ seitú , if æè hast nede of helpe , axe it of
thyne frendes ,

For æäe isnysisnys noon so good a Phisicien , as thy trewe
frend

¢ And after this , tha~ne shuln ©e kepe ©ow , fro alle stunge
folk , & fro lyeres , & haue alwey in suspect here compaignye ,

For Piers Alfonce seitú , Ne take no compaignye by the weye
of a straunge man , but if so be , æ æè haue knowe hi~ of a lengerö
tyme ,

And if so be , æ he falle in to thy compaignye , ¹auen-
ture , w outen thyn assent ,

enquere tha~ne as subtilly as æè
maist , of his co~uäsaciou~ , & of his lyf byfore ¢ and feyne thy
wey , sey æ æè wolt go thider , as æè wolt nat go ,
and if he beretú
